campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met in early September to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and where money was spent that month. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as better connections between different types of transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city money management
July 2025 treasurer's financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit options and connectivity

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to community events and organizations. They approved funding decisions for secured grants, set aside money for competitive event grants in the upcoming fiscal year, and made updates to the rules for how groups can apply for money to host events or close streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations and dollar amounts for secured grant funding
Set budget allocation for the Competitive Event Grant Program
Updated rules and clarifications for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This was a San José City Council meeting held on March 25, 2026. The agenda focused on providing access information for residents who wanted to watch the meeting, including live translation services in over 50 languages and multiple ways to tune in.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple viewing options: cable, online streaming, and phone access
Meeting broadcast on City of San José YouTube and website
Zoom webinar access for remote participation

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council cancelled the zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026. The hearing has been rescheduled for April 8, 2026, so if you had plans to speak about zoning issues or property changes in the city, you'll need to mark your calendar for the later date instead.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal funding that helps pay for community development projects and affordable housing in Sunnyvale. The group approved meeting notes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking grants to support housing and services for residents in need.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development grants being awarded to local organizations
Review of previous meeting minutes from February
Presentations from groups applying for funding to help with housing and human services

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses get their licenses in order, plans to encourage shopping at local stores, and ways the city can partner with private companies to support retail shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help shops get compliant
Outreach efforts targeting shopping centers in Cupertino
Summer Shop Local campaign to boost local retail
City sponsorship of events through partnerships with private companies
New incentives to attract and support small businesses and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they addressed several postponed agenda items. The council voted to approve a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of the county's annual weed abatement program, and residents had a chance to comment on how the city plans to spend federal housing grant money for the 2026-27 fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed removal program across Santa Clara neighborhoods
Federal HUD housing grant funding decisions for next fiscal year
Multiple agenda items delayed or reconsidered from previous meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including a proclamation honoring Women's History Month, a partnership agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda item added to discussion
